title: podman: add support for splitting imagestore using `--imagestore`
body:
Add support for `--imagestore` in podman which allows users to split the filesystem of containers vs image store, imagestore if configured will pull images in image storage instead of the graphRoot while keeping the other parts still in the originally configured graphRoot.

This is an implementation of
https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1549 in podman.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
podman: add support for splitting imagestore using `--imagestore`
```

comments.body: /hold wait for the upstream PR to get merged.
please squash the 3rd commit in the vendor commit, so that test are still passing if we bisect
PR is ready for review.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL again.
Unsure but looks like latest `c/storage` might be causing some issues, checking here: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18534

> Unsure but looks like latest `c/storage` might be causing some issues, checking here: #18534

yes it is not working, also see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18510

@flouthoc I gave a testing for this PR, but I have a question need to confirm firstly, Need I setup imagestore configuration in /etc/containers/storage.conf and specify --imagestore option for podman together? if I only run podman with --imagestore option, I can't see pulled image by ./bin/podman images, is it an expected result? for details as follows.

```
[cloud-user@rhel-9 podman]$ git log -1
commit ce23346370126823edc5ba77b5a5b0f0e9126750 (HEAD -> PR#18224)
Author: Aditya R <arajan@redhat.com>
Date:   Mon May 8 15:34:05 2023 +0530

    podman: add support for splitting imagestore
    
    Add support for `--imagestore` in podman which allows users to split the filesystem of containers vs image store, imagestore if configured will pull images in image storage instead of the graphRoot while keeping the other parts still in the originally configured graphRoot.
    
    This is an implementation of
    https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1549 in podman.
    
    Signed-off-by: Aditya R <arajan@redhat.com>
```

@chuanchang 

Regarding question 1
* Your `podman images` command is missing `--imagestore` option hence its showing images only from graphroot

Regarding question 2
* Could you make sure that pulling `ubuntu` works when using `cli` flag ? Also verify for user `root` vs `rootful`

I tried similar scenario with a usb stick on my system and it works fine

@chuanchang For 

Q1 and Q2 
Yes podman will show image from both `imagestore` and `graphroot` but while pulling it will store the image in the `imagestore` as configured. Reason is we only want user to add new images to `imagestore` and use images from both `graphroot` and the original `imagestore`.

Q3
If it works with `cli` flag `--imagestore` and not with configured `imagestore` in `storage.conf` it could be an issue with the `storage.conf`.
I think your `rootless` podman is not using `/etc/containers/storage.conf` but using the configuration configured for user, you can verify that by doing `podman info` it will show which configuration file it is using.  Could you share output of `podman info` and content of the `storage.conf` which it is using here ?

@flouthoc yes, the default storage configuration file is  /home/cloud-user/.config/containers/storage.conf and no such file.

```
[cloud-user@rhel-9 podman]$ ./bin/podman info|grep storage
  configFile: /home/cloud-user/.config/containers/storage.conf
  graphRoot: /home/cloud-user/.local/share/containers/storage
  volumePath: /home/cloud-user/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

[cloud-user@rhel-9 podman]$ cat /home/cloud-user/.config/containers/storage.conf
cat: /home/cloud-user/.config/containers/storage.conf: No such file or directory
```
@chuanchang Yes then you will need to create a storage.conf which podman is using :)
@containers/podman-maintainers Merge me pls, requested `c/storage` for the feature is already vendor-ed.

@chuanchang I found the issue in your`storage.conf` which is causing the issue `imagestore` option should be under `[storage]` and not under `[storage.options]` , field is on the samelevel as `graphroot` in storage.conf. Could you amend your `storage.conf` and try again. Thanks.

> @chuanchang I found the issue in your`storage.conf` which is causing the issue `imagestore` option should be under `[storage]` and not under `[storage.options]` , field is on the samelevel as `graphroot` in storage.conf. Could you amend your `storage.conf` and try again. Thanks.
